items.headline: Yankees slugger Giancarlo Stanton (calf) out of lineup vs. Astros and could land on injured list
items.description:
HOUSTON (AP) — New York Yankees slugger Giancarlo Stanton was not in the starting lineup Saturday night against Houston after exiting Friday’s win over the Astros because of right calf tightness.
Yankees manager Aaron Boone said it's possible Stanton will be placed on the injured list.

items.headline: The struggling Boston Red Sox have fired manager Alex Cora and 5 members of his coaching staff
items.description: BOSTON (AP) — The struggling Boston Red Sox have fired manager Alex Cora and 5 members of his coaching staff.

items.headline: Schmidt, Gilbert and Ramos hit home runs as the Giants beat the Marlins 6-2
items.description:
SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Casey Schmidt hit a two-run homer, Drew Gilbert and Heliot Ramos added solo shots and the San Francisco Giants beat the Miami Marlins 6-2 on Saturday.
The Marlins took an early 1-0 lead on Xavier Edwards’ RBI single off Giants starter Robbie Ray with two outs in the third before Gilbert tied it in the fifth with his homer off Miami starter Eury Pérez, his second of the season.

items.headline: Boston Red Sox fire manager Alex Cora and 5 coaches
items.description:
BOSTON (AP) — The Boston Red Sox have fired manager Alex Cora, who led them to the 2018 World Series championship.
The team made the announcement Saturday after a 17-1 victory over the Baltimore Orioles that snapped a four-game losing streak — including a three-game sweep at Fenway Park by the archrival New York Yankees.

items.headline: Nelly Korda slows her torrid pace and settles for 5-shot lead in Chevron Championship
items.description:
HOUSTON (AP) — Nelly Korda tied the 54-hole scoring record at The Chevron Championship on Saturday. She had a five-shot lead going into the final round, the largest of her career. A victory would take her back to No. 1 in the women's world ranking.
And she headed straight for the putting green.

items.headline: Fitzpatrick brothers post a tournament-record 57 and take a 4-shot lead in the Zurich Classic
items.description:
AVONDALE, La. (AP) — Matt Fitzpatrick and his younger brother Alex shot a tournament-record 15-under 57 in better-ball play on Saturday, giving them a four-shot lead heading into the final round of the Zurich Classic.
The Englishmen were at 30-under 186 through 54 holes of the PGA Tour's only team event.

items.headline: Shane McClanahan wins at Tropicana Field for 1st time in nearly 3 years as Rays blank Twins 6-0
items.description:
ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Shane McClanahan turned in five scoreless innings to get his first win at Tropicana Field in nearly three years as the Tampa Bay Rays blanked the Minnesota Twins 6-0 on Saturday.
The left-hander, returning from two surgeries that cost him the 2024 and 2025 seasons, scattered three hits and struck out seven.

items.headline: Gilgeous-Alexander scores 42, Thunder beat Suns 121-109 to take a 3-0 series lead
items.description:
PHOENIX (AP) — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 42 points in a brilliant shooting performance, Ajay Mitchell added 15 in place of the injured Jalen Williams and the Oklahoma City Thunder beat the Phoenix Suns 121-109 on Saturday to take a 3-0 lead in the Western Conference first-round series.
Gilgeous-Alexander — the league's reigning MVP — hit 15 of 18 shots on a variety of tough looks, frustrating a Suns defense that actually played decently for much of the game. Alex Caruso added 13 points off the bench while Chet Holmgren had 10 points, seven rebounds and two blocks.

items.headline: Sunday's NBA matchups: Spurs-Trail Blazers, Celtics-76ers, Lakers-Rockets, Cavaliers-Raptors
items.description:
The San Antonio Spurs' kids look ready, even if Victor Wembanyama may not be.
With the Spurs star unavailable, second-year guard Stephon Castle delivered 33 points and rookie Dylan Harper pitched in a career-high 27 points along with 10 rebounds in a 120-108 victory in Portland on Friday to take a 2-1 series lead.

items.headline: Stankoven scores again, Hurricanes complete 4-game sweep of Senators
items.description:
OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — Logan Stankoven scored the go-ahead goal in the third period as the Carolina Hurricanes defeated Ottawa 4-2 to sweep the first-round series.
Taylor Hall added a goal and an assist before Sebastian Aho buried two pucks into the empty net. Frederik Andersen made 25 saves. Seth Jarvis chipped in two assists.
